schema.testingAndDeployment: Record the agreed testing strategy and deployment arrangement in readme.projectSummary. If testing, rollout, or deployment needs explicit execution sequencing, add dedicated features for them.

projectSummary.goal: Plan a Kali-like NixOS VM for local Web-security learning within this existing flake repository, validate it locally first, then add a flake app for fast launch.
scope: Define the VM host direction, Web-security tooling scope, Kali-like X11/Xfce desktop direction, local test path, and app-wrapper path.
Produce implementation-ready planning artifacts after clarification.
Split suitable user-facing tools into Home Manager while keeping system-critical runtime pieces in NixOS.
constraints: Planner role only; no implementation or machine configuration in this stage.
Implementation should prefer Kali-like defaults where practical.
Validation must check both system-level and Home Manager-installed software.
Acceptance must run in two explicit phases: NixOS acceptance first, then Home Manager acceptance.
testingStrategy: Build the local VM with `nix build .#nixosConfigurations.sec-lab.config.system.build.vm`.
Run the generated launcher locally and verify that the guest reaches an X11 + Xfce session.
First-pass NixOS acceptance: verify the expected system packages are installed and executable before relying on Home Manager-delivered tools.
Second-pass Home Manager acceptance: verify the Home Manager profile for user `sec` is present and its packages are installed and executable.
Verify the flake app `nix run .#vm-sec-lab` launches the same VM path after app wrapping is added.
deploymentPlan: No remote deployment target is requested in the current phase.
Implementation should focus on a local-only VM workflow.
After the VM build path works, expose a local quick-start app entrypoint in flake outputs under `vm-sec-lab`.
notes: The repository should keep the VM host identity aligned on `sec-lab` with user `sec`.
User prefers Kali-like defaults where practical.
Default tooling scope is Web security.
User wants the default set to include `hydra`.
User also wants a Metasploit suite package in the default image.
Current default Web-tool direction includes Burp Suite, ZAP, sqlmap, ffuf, httpx, nuclei, whatweb, nikto, gobuster, dirsearch, hydra, Metasploit, and related support tools.
Tool placement should be interleaved into two layers: NixOS base tools first, then Home Manager user tools.
Initial NixOS-layer recommendation: X11/Xfce stack, Firefox, Wireshark, tcpdump, curl, wget, nmap, python3, git, Burp Suite, ZAP, Metasploit.
Initial Home Manager-layer recommendation: jq, pipx, tmux, neovim, sqlmap, ffuf, httpx, nuclei, whatweb, nikto, gobuster, dirsearch, hydra.

features.title: Define sec-lab host layout
features.description: Add or migrate the repository host definitions so the VM target is represented as host `sec-lab` with user `sec`, including the corresponding NixOS, disko, factor, and Home Manager paths.
acceptance: `flake.nix` exposes `nixosConfigurations.sec-lab` and `homeConfigurations.sec@sec-lab`.
The repository contains host-specific paths for `sec-lab` as the final VM identity.
The host remains compatible with the repository's existing multi-host flake structure.

features.title: Create Kali-like X11 and Xfce VM base
features.description: Define the VM-oriented NixOS base for `sec-lab`, including X11, Xfce, suitable VM defaults, networking, and Kali-like usability choices where practical on NixOS.
acceptance: The `sec-lab` NixOS configuration builds a VM derivation successfully.
The VM can boot to a usable X11 + Xfce session.
The user `sec` exists and can log into the desktop session.

features.title: Install the Web-security toolkit with system and Home Manager split
features.description: Add the agreed default toolkit: Burp Suite, ZAP, sqlmap, ffuf, httpx, nuclei, whatweb, nikto, gobuster, dirsearch, hydra, Metasploit, wireshark, tcpdump, curl, wget, jq, python3, pipx, git, tmux, and neovim. Keep system-critical runtime pieces in NixOS and place suitable user-facing tools in Home Manager where that improves manageability.
acceptance: The final package split between NixOS and Home Manager is documented in code comments or module structure clearly enough for maintenance.
The guest system contains the agreed default Web-security toolkit after activation.
At least some user-facing tools are managed through `home-manager/sec-lab/sec/` instead of placing the entire toolkit in the system profile.
The tool split is arranged to support separate NixOS acceptance and Home Manager acceptance.

Unless package constraints force a change, the initial split should follow: NixOS for Firefox, Burp Suite, ZAP, Metasploit, Wireshark, tcpdump, curl, wget, nmap, python3, git, and desktop/runtime components; Home Manager for jq, pipx, tmux, neovim, sqlmap, ffuf, httpx, nuclei, whatweb, nikto, gobuster, dirsearch, and hydra.

features.title: Run NixOS-layer acceptance
features.description: Verify the built `sec-lab` VM at the NixOS layer first: boot, desktop/session, networking basics, and the base tool subset that must exist before Home Manager validation.
acceptance: The VM boots successfully into the expected X11 + Xfce environment.
Representative NixOS-layer tools are present and executable, such as `wireshark`, `tcpdump`, `curl`, `wget`, `nmap`, and any other tools intentionally assigned to the system profile.
The NixOS acceptance result is recorded before Home Manager acceptance begins.

features.title: Run Home Manager-layer acceptance
features.description: After the NixOS layer passes, verify the `sec` user's Home Manager profile and the user-scoped Web tools assigned there.
acceptance: The Home Manager profile for `sec@sec-lab` is present and activated in the guest.
Representative Home Manager-layer tools are present and executable, such as user-scoped terminal tooling and any Web tools intentionally assigned to the home profile.
Home Manager acceptance only runs after the NixOS acceptance feature passes.

features.title: Expose quick-start app vm-sec-lab
features.description: Add a flake app named `vm-sec-lab` that launches the locally built `sec-lab` VM through a stable command path.
acceptance: `nix run .#vm-sec-lab` starts the `sec-lab` VM launch path.
The app implementation reuses the same VM target as `.#nixosConfigurations.sec-lab.config.system.build.vm` rather than a divergent code path.

features.title: Verify local build, launch, and installed tools
features.description: Run the agreed local validation flow end to end: build the VM, launch it, complete NixOS acceptance, complete Home Manager acceptance, and confirm the `vm-sec-lab` app works.
acceptance: The VM build command completes successfully for `sec-lab`.
The generated VM launcher starts successfully and reaches the expected guest environment.
Package verification is explicitly split into a NixOS acceptance pass and a Home Manager acceptance pass.
`nix run .#vm-sec-lab` is verified after the app wrapper is added.
